name: Chicken Biryani

hero.caption: chicken biryani — dum pot, saffron rice

healthFacts: Aged basmati has a lower glycemic index than regular white rice — a slower glucose spike.
Whole spices (cloves, cinnamon, cardamom) are anti-inflammatory — not just for flavor.
Saffron contains crocin, studied for antioxidant activity and mood support.
Chicken provides ~25g protein per serving in this recipe.
Caramelized birista (fried onions) develop Maillard antioxidants and deep umami.
Mint and coriander in biryani act as natural digestive aids for a rich meal.

utensils.name: Large heavy-bottomed pot with tight lid

steps.title: Make the birista
steps.detail: Slice onions thin and even. Deep-fry in oil on medium heat, stirring constantly, until deep golden-brown and crispy. Drain on paper — they crisp up more as they cool. Use half for marinade, half for layering.
steps.tip: Don't rush. Pale onions = no flavor. Burnt = bitter. Deep golden-brown is the target.

steps.title: Marinate the chicken
steps.detail: Mix yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, Kashmiri chili, biryani masala, lemon juice, salt, and half the crushed birista. Score the chicken and coat deeply. Marinate for at least 1 hour — 4 hours is the real move.
steps.tip: Yogurt + lemon acid breaks down the meat fibers. More time = noticeably more tender.

steps.title: Parboil the rice
steps.detail: Soak rice 30 minutes. Boil a large pot of very salty water (like pasta water) with whole spices. Cook rice until exactly 70% done — grains should have a firm, chalky center when pressed. Drain immediately.
steps.tip: 70% cooked = breaks with slight resistance, not a snap. Watch closely — it moves fast.

steps.title: Layer the biryani
steps.detail: Heavy pot: spread raw marinated chicken at the base. Add half the parboiled rice. Scatter mint, coriander, and half the birista. Drizzle saffron milk and 2 tbsp ghee. Repeat with remaining rice, herbs, ghee, and saffron on top.
steps.tip: Saffron goes on top — it slowly seeps down, creating yellow-orange streaks through the rice.

steps.title: Dum cook (steam seal)
steps.detail: Seal the pot with foil pressed tight, then place the lid. Cook on high heat for 5 minutes to build steam pressure, then drop to the absolute lowest heat setting for 25–30 minutes. Do not lift the lid during dum.
steps.tip: Place a flat tawa under the pot to diffuse heat and protect the bottom layer.

steps.title: Rest, unseal, and serve
steps.detail: Turn off heat. Let rest sealed for 10 minutes — the steam continues to cook gently. Unseal carefully (hot steam). Mix gently from the sides with a wide spatula, folding rather than stirring. Serve immediately.
steps.tip: Fold gently — biryani rice should be separate grains, never clumped or broken.
